Compared to other public heavy asset managers with mutual fund structures, T. Rowe Price shares a similar valuation with Franklin Resources (BEN) and Invesco (IVZ), trading at 11.92x price-to-forward earnings. Yet, TROW has many appealing characteristics compared to the rest of the peer group, with an attractive net income margin of 28.38%, an enviable return on equity of 18.45%, which vastly outperforms the 2.80% from Franklin Resources and 5.09% of Invesco. Lastly, all three public asset managers have faced headwinds over the past three years, which has led to a decline in their bottom line. Nonetheless, T. Rowe Price has declined by much less than BEN and IVZ, which allows them to have a more appealing valuation when considering their fundamentals.

Furthermore, compared to the past years, the forward PE valuation of T. Rowe Price is more appealing than the historical average, as it sits -10.6% below. At the same time, the dividend yield of 5.02% is higher than the 4.45% average, suggesting that T. Rowe Price is undervalued.

Takeaway

With the market recovery, the shares of T. Rowe Price are more appealing than before, as most of its funds charge daily, which facilitates faster recovery from the market turmoil. TROW could be considered a business with strong profitability margins, as well as robust liquidity and leverage metrics, but it lacks growth and is facing headwinds in the popularity of its core product. Nonetheless, although the company has been exhibiting constant outflows due to these headwinds, assets under management haven't dropped as asset levels appreciate, and therefore, the revenue still grows. Of course, I do not find this to be a strong business as it is in a decaying industry; nonetheless, their solid business income statement and balance sheet support a "hold" rating in this analysis.

data.title: Available Information Indicates That The Merger Between ANSYS And Synopsys Will Proceed As Planned
data.description:
The ANSYS and Synopsys Investment Thesis

ANSYS, Inc. (NASDAQ:ANSS) and Synopsys, Inc. (NASDAQ:SNPS) are currently in the process of obtaining the necessary approvals from several countries for the planned merger to take place. And now, I think, an important point has come to their advantage, which will probably increase the likelihood of an upcoming approval by China.

Since only China's approval is pending, I strongly believe that the merger will be completed as planned soon.

Reviewing My Previous Coverage of the Merger

I became aware of Synopsys because it dominates the EDA market alongside Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) and is the second strongest force in the IP design market after ARM Holdings (ARM). Since both markets present significant barriers to entry, such as the required knowledge, and since Synopsys has many different customers, I consider this business model and its ability to generate revenue to be very robust.

After the press releases about the planned merger with ANSYS came out, I took a look at it, and I view the subsequent merger with ANSYS very positively because it will result in an end-to-end EDA solution that focuses on AI. In my opinion, this is a forward-thinking move. Unlike competitors, who usually only cover individual areas, Synopsys and ANSYS as a result, can focus on the entire value chain. This should give them a competitive advantage.

After Synopsys presented its guidance for fiscal year 2025, which was smaller than I expected, I had to adjust my valuation. For this reason, in December 2024, I wrote an article titled "The Downside When Stocks Are Priced to the Point of Perfection." Both articles concluded that Synopsys is an exceptional company, but its high valuation poses a risk.

A few days later, in late December 2024, I issued another update because the UK Antitrust Authority had become involved to ensure continued fair competition. Based on my assumptions, I thought the UK authorities would approve the deal rather smoothly and that the planned divestments would satisfy them. This has turned out to be true.

In my last article about Synopsys, published in May 2025, I predicted that the FY guidance would likely be lowered due to the increased uncertainty caused by tariffs. Although Synopsys did not lower its guidance, the company suspended its FY guidance after releasing its Q2 results, as an accurate forecast was not possible due to the challenging business environment.

What Has Happened Since Then?

The most important news was probably that, except for the Chinese, all jurisdictions gave their approval for the merger. And that there are also advanced talks with the Chinese authorities to receive final approval from them.

Due to the tariff situation, obtaining such approval could have been difficult. Therefore, it was convenient that Synopsys and Cadence received approval to do business in China again a few days later. I believe this approval increases the likelihood that the Chinese regulatory authority will approve the merger. Therefore, there is a good chance that Synopsys and ANSYS will receive approval within the next few weeks.

It is also important that the decision is made relatively soon, as the deadline for the merger deal is January 15, 2026, so in about six months. If the deal is not finalized by then, there is a chance that it will not come to fruition. But it seems that the planned timeframe for the deal, which was scheduled for sometime in 2025, will be met.

What Is My Best Guess About the Current Situation, and What Are the Implications of That Guess?

I think the proposed divestments are plausible and the core business areas of ANSYS and Synopsys will continue to exist. Keysight Technologies (KEYS) could benefit as well, as it will receive the divested assets from ANSYS and Synopsys. This will likely strengthen Keysight's competitive position.

Furthermore, the end of export restrictions to China should lead to the resumption of guidance in the next quarter, allowing Synopsys to achieve higher sales and profits than expected by analysts for fiscal year 2025. Therefore, I see the developments for Synopsys as positive.

Additionally, I think the arbitrage opportunity that existed a few months ago is now very small, as the likelihood of the deal going through is relatively high.

Risks

Since the deal is structured so that ANSYS shareholders will receive $197 per share and an additional 0.345 Synopsys shares, it will likely dilute the shareholders. Since both companies have relatively high SBC costs, I expect the number of outstanding shares to increase significantly in the next few years. Therefore, it would not surprise me if the combined company had more than 200 million outstanding shares in the near future. Once the deal is finalized, Synopsys' outstanding shares are likely to increase from ~150 million to ~180 million.

The second question, of course, is whether the planned synergies will actually be as large as they are currently planned. Often, synergies are overestimated. The TAM could also be lower than expected, as all forecasts for the future always carry the risk of misjudgment.

Since the deal naturally includes a mix of debt, the debt situation will initially worsen. However, the management has stated that the deleveraging should be completed within two years, resulting in a debt-to-adjusted EBITDA ratio of less than 2x. I consider this a reasonable debt situation as long as the deleveraging succeeds.

Valuation

Currently, Synopsys is trading at a lower multiple than Cadence, as the market apparently considers Cadence to be safer due to the merger situation and the still pending approval by China. However, if the merger is carried out as planned, I see the possibility of Synopsys/ANSYS reaching Cadence's valuation through a multiple expansion. Because I believe the new, combined Synopsys/ANSYS company will be stronger than Cadence. The combination of EDA, design IP, and simulation should provide a significant competitive advantage.

Since I can't create an updated five-year forecast without the latest guidance, I'm referring to my previous article, in which I calculated the previous upside. But as soon as Synopsys provides a new forecast, I will update it.

Conclusion

I strongly believe that the merger deadline of January 15, 2026, will be met, and Synopsys and ANSYS are well on their way to having this transaction approved in China. It will be interesting to see the initial forecasts of the new company and how management estimates future sales and profit growth. I believe the new company will play a crucial role in the future development of AI. Therefore, I continue to view the two companies as attractive investments, as I expect them to exceed expectations, which will likely increase the multiple.

data.title: S&P 500: The Melt-Up Stage, Bust Approaching
data.description:
The S&P500 (SP500) is currently in the melt-up stage after the resilient first half of the year, and, based on my macro analysis, a major selloff is likely in the second half of the year.

Excess Optimism Entering 2025

First, it's important to understand the expectations for 2025 towards the end of 2024.

After the November election, investors and businesses were exceedingly exceeded about Trump's victory. Thus, due to the widespread optimism, the expectations were high for 2025.

For example, the S&P500 earnings for 2025 were expected to grow at around 14% in December 2024, and the Fed was predicting a solid growth with falling inflation. The optimism was particularly strong in small businesses, as shown in the chart below.

The excess optimism was based on the expectations that Trump's administration is possibly the most pro-growth administration ever, with the focus on deregulation and tax cuts - clearly ignoring expected negative effects of policies such as tariffs, and immigration.

DeepSeek And A Possible AI Bubble Burst

The bull market in the S&P500 since November 2022 was led by the tech mega-caps associated with the AI trade, due to a massive AI capex.

However, the DeepSeek event in January raised the possibility that heavy AI capex is not necessary, as the Chinese lab developed a dominant reasoning model at only a fraction of cost.

Thus, at that point, the expectation was the AI capex might have to be reduced - and this would burst the AI bubble. The DeepSeek event started to curb the post-election optimism.

The Stagflationary Warning And The Crash

In addition, as Trump took office, it became clearer that the early focus will be on tariffs and immigration policies, thus the market became more concerned about the stagflationary effects of these policies.

The PMI flesh report on February 21st was the first soft-data evidence of stagflation - and this marked the top of the bull market.

The stagflationary worries exploded on April 2nd Liberation Day, when Trump unveiled the punitive reciprocal tariffs, which caused the S&P500 to fall by excess of 20% to around 4800 level on intraday futures level. The retaliatory tariffs on China, which reached 145%, added to the shock.

The quick bear market drawdown was based on a deflating AI bubble, and the expected stagflationary effects of Trump policies. By April, the post-election optimism had nearly evaporated.

The TACO Trade And The Bounce

However, Trump quickly issued a 90-day delay on reciprocal tariffs, and the markets started to view the threat of tariffs as a negotiating tool. The baseline expectation was that the likely tariff policy will be a 10% universal tariff with some sector-specific tariffs.

Thus, the stock markets bottomed out and started to bounce, expecting that the baseline tariff scenario would not cause stagflation. Note, this scenario assumes that the US makes the trade deals with all trading partners by July 9th.

The bounce accelerated with the US-China trade truce, and opening of the 90-day negotiating window.

The AI Capex Stays Strong

Investors were anxiously waiting for the Q1 earnings from mega-cap tech stocks, with close attention to their AI capex plans, given the DeepSeek event. The Q1 earnings were mostly solid, and none of the major AI players lowered the Capex plans for 2025.

Microsoft (MSFT) Q1 earnings were particularly strong, and Microsoft lead the market higher, which boosted other AI stocks. Thus, the AI trade reflated as the AI bubble burst worries evaporated, and this also accelerated the bounce.

Geopolitics - No Spike In Oil

Turmp also gave Iran 60 days to negotiate the nuclear deal. As soon as the negotiation period expired with no deal, Israel attacked Iran's nuclear and military infrastructure. Iran threatened to close the Strait of Hurmuz, and cause a major spike in oil price (CL1:COM). However, after the US bombed the Iranian underground nuclear facilities, Iran failed to retaliate substantially, which allowed for a temporary cease fire, with oil falling back to mid-60s.

This was the event that triggered the melt-up, as the S&P500 broke out to new all-time highs in response.

The Meltup

Thus, the S&P500 recovered all losses in 2025, and it is currently reaching new highs in what seems to be a major melt-up.

In summary, these are the reasons for the melt-up:

  1. Expectations that all trade deals will be negotiated, and that tariffs in place will not cause stagflation.
  2. Expectations that the restrictive immigration policy will not be stagflationary.
  3. Expectations that AI capex will remain strong, and that the AI stocks continue to beat the earnings estimates.
  4. Expectations that Israel-Iran ceasefire holds, and thus, the price of oil stays low.

In addition:

  1. The US Congress has passed Trump's tax bill, and the expectations are possibly shifting towards the pro-growth agenda.
  2. Trump is pressuring the Fed to cut interest rates, and the possibility of a shadow Fed chair could turn the Fed more dovish.

What's Coming In The Second Half Of The Year?

The Bubble

First, it is important to note that the S&P500 is trading at a bubble-like valuation.

The Shiller PE ratio is above 38, which is the second-highest value ever, only behind 44 in January 2000 at the height of the dot-com bubble. The 2025 forward PE ratio is at above 24.

At the same time, the earnings estimates keep declining. Currently, the consensus estimate for 2025 is 6.8% growth, well below the 14% growth estimate at the end of 2024.

Thus, the risk is asymmetric, it's unrealistic to expect a further P/E multiple expansion, so the upside is limited, while any negative news can burst the bubble and cause a deep selloff.

The Trade Deals

The trade deal with Vietnam could be a template for other trade deals. Vietnam has agreed to a 20% tariff, with 40% tariff of transshipping items.

First, the 10% universal tariff assumption is proving wrong, as Vietnam is paying 20%, so tariffs are only going higher, not as high as the Liberation Day level, but above the 10% level. This is bad news, as it worsens the stagflation prospects.

Second, the US will try to contain China with trade deals, as the 40% transshipping tariff is likely directed at China. This is bad news, because 1) it means higher tariffs, and 2) trade war escalation with China.

Waiting For The Hard Data To Confirm Stagflation

Most importantly, during the second half of the year we will start seeing the net effects of Trump policies on hard data - employment, growth, and inflation.

The melt-up assumption is that growth stays sold and inflation stays contained. In my opinion, we will see an inflationary recession. The first sign of stagflation in the official hard data is likely to burst the bubble, and trigger a deep bear market in the S&P500.

Geopolitics

The big question is whether Iran still has the capabilities to develop a nuclear weapon. If the assessment shows that the damage to Iran's nuclear program was limited, Israel might be force to attack Iran again. Thus, the oil price shock is still a possibility in H2.

Implications

The markets have been resilient in H1 2025, but only because the hard data has not reflected the net effects of Trump policies yet - and this is because the hard data is lagging, and there was a significant frontloading before the tariffs kicked in.

I expect that the hard data will start to deteriorate during H2 2025. Specifically, I expect that consumption will sharply slow, due to: 1) resumption of student loan payments, 2) negative net-migration due to immigration policy, which is a demand shock, 3) higher prices and 4) slowing labor market. Eventually, this will trigger a recession, most likely in Q3.

The labor market is already slowing. In May, all jobs were created in Health/Social Services, and in June, all jobs were created in Health/Social Services and Government. That's weak. I expect that job growth will turn negative in Q3, but the unemployment rate might not rise above 4.5%, as the labor force shrinks due to immigration policies.

Higher prices mean higher inflation, and this will be mainly due to tariffs.

The Fed will be in a difficult situation, where the unemployment rate remains below 4.5%, but the labor market is weak, growth is negative, but also inflation is rising. Thus, the Fed will be limited in its ability to cut interest rates significantly, beyond the signaled 2 cuts in 2025.

Thus, I expect that the stock market bubble will burst in the second half of 2025, and it will be primarily in response to the negative growth data. As the growth turns negative, the tech companies are unlikely to keep beating the estimates - and this will likely burst the AI bubble.

The melt-up could continue until the first evidence of stagflation, but I would not chase it. Rather, I would sell into it.

data.title: FreightCar America: Strong Demand For Railcars Plus Margin Gains
data.description:
FreightCar America (NASDAQ:RAIL) has multiple positives going for it. The company shifted production to a much lower-cost area. This should drive significant margin increases, leading to strong earnings growth. The stock is undervalued, leaving a lot of room to the upside.

FreightCar America's Business Background

FreightCar America is a $171 million market cap company. FreightCar America designs and manufactures a variety of freight cars with various lengths and cubic feet including: Intermodal & non-intermodal flat cars, box cars, open & closed top hoppers, and gondolas. These rail cars are used to transport a variety of dry-bulk products. The capacities of the cars range from 3,282 to 6,500 cubic feet.

RAIL also rebuilds and converts railcars and sells forged/cast/fabricated parts for all of the cars that the company produces and for those made by other companies. FreightCar sells its railcars in North America and exports to the Middle East and Latin America. The company alters its manufacturing specs based on the regions where the cars will be used, as track gauges are sized differently outside of North America.

FreightCar America tends to have long-term relationships with its customers. These customers include: railroads, North American shippers, and financial institutions. The company maintains these long-term relationships by producing high-quality products with engineering design expertise & technological leadership. RAIL's leadership has the company producing innovative railcars at competitive prices.

FreightCar America's Growth Catalysts

FreightCar America has many positive growth catalysts. One of these is FreightCar's manufacturing facility located in Coahuila, Mexico. The company is achieving significant operating efficiencies at this facility, which is driving margin growth. The gross margin increased 780 basis points to 14.9% in Q1 2025. This drove a 19.7% gain in adjusted EBITDA to $7.3 million in Q1 2025 as compared to $6.1 million from Q1 2024.

The gains achieved for the gross margin and adjusted EBITDA reflect the operational efficiencies that RAIL is achieving at its Mexico plant. This facility is vertically integrated, which allows for quick flexibility and customizations to provide what its customers need. The plant can produce over 5,000 railcars annually. At the same time, RAIL operates the plant with high efficiency, keeping costs down. The plant is strategically located near the U.S. border, which reduces supply chain delays and transit times.

RAIL also experienced a gain of 1,250 new railcar orders in Q1. This brings the total backlog to 3,337 railcars, equating to $318 million. This is 61% higher than the backlog of 2,075 railcars from Q1 2024. The higher backlog should help drive future revenue and earnings growth.

FreightCar America is demonstrating leadership in the industry as RAIL is the fastest growing railcar manufacturer in North America according to ARCI data. RAIL increased its addressable market share by 8% to 27% from Q1 2024 through Q1 2025. This achievement shows that RAIL is competing effectively for quality, reliability, and value as it is the go-to railcar producer in North America.

The company stated that it continues to get a strong pace of customer inquiries for ongoing additional railcar orders. RAIL expects that industry-wide deliveries will pick up momentum as the year progresses.

RAIL's deliveries are expected to ramp up significantly in the second half of 2025. The company reaffirmed its full year 2025 guidance for 4,500 to 4,900 railcar deliveries for revenue of $530 million to $595 million. RAIL projected adjusted EBITDA for 2025 to be $43 million to $49 million. The company achieved $43 million in adjusted EBITDA in 2024. So, if RAIL achieves $49 million in adjusted EBITDA in 2025, it would be about 14% growth.

RAIL is poised to benefit from the long-term industry demand for 35,000 to 42,000 railcars per year. The increasing demand for railcar freight is driving ongoing demand for new railcars. This is a result of the expansion of global trade and the economy. RAIL provides a great cost-effective way to move large quantities of goods such as coal, agriculture products, chemicals, minerals, etc. via its innovative railcars.

Valuation

FreightCar America is trading attractively with a forward EV/EBITDA of 5.95. This is significantly lower than the sector median forward EV/EBITDA of 11.65. Analysts are projecting RAIL to achieve EBITDA of $46.42 million in 2025. This would be an 8% gain over 2024. This low valuation leaves room for the stock to move higher as RAIL continues its growth.

The targeted EBITDA can be achieved with about $565 million in revenue growth along with the margin increases that the company has been achieving. The 26% gain in gross margin Q1 2025 over Q1 2024 is a great start to the year. We'll have to see how the remaining quarters of 2025 look to see how the full year will turn out.

Technical Perspective

RAIL's weekly chart above shows the stock recovering over the past 4 weeks from a sizable 73% drop from about $16 to about $4.40. The RSI is now in the bullish zone above the 50 level, but still well below the overbought level of 70. The MACD looks flat, as the blue MACD line is about even with the red signal line. The RSI and MACD have more room to move higher before frothy conditions exist. The stock did move deep into overbought territory (above 80) on the RSI at the end of 2024 before the stock sold off. So, it can do the same if the news and the company sentiment remain positive.

The next earnings report for Q2 2025 on August 4, 2025, could be the next catalyst for the stock. Analysts are projecting RAIL to achieve about $115 million in revenue and normalized EPS of $0.06 for the quarter. Meeting or exceeding these estimates plus continued margin expansion and backlog growth could spark the stock to make further gains.

The Q2 earnings report can provide insights into RAIL's strengths and momentum. It should also include the company's perspective on how the rest of the year is likely to go. Positive guidance from the company can help provide a boost for the stock.

Risks for the Stock

If FreightCar America significantly misses analysts' estimates or misses its own targets, it could have a negative impact on the stock. Investors will have to see how the company reports in future quarters to gauge the likely direction for the stock.

RAIL has a high amount of total debt of $158.3 million in relation to total cash of $53.5 million, leaving the company with net debt of $104.8 million. Standout debt items include: $105 million in long-term debt and $46 million in long-term finance lease liabilities. The high amount of debt could be an issue if the company fell on bad times with a period of negative cash flow.

Another weakness on the balance sheet is that there are 1.4x more total liabilities than total assets, giving RAIL negative total equity of ($97.4 million). This could put the company at risk of meeting its debt obligations if RAIL had negative cash flow for a sustained period. The good news is that FreightCar America had positive operating cash flow of $83 million for the trailing 12 months. The company could strengthen its balance sheet with continued cash flow growth, paying off more debt, and increasing assets such as inventory and accounts receivable.

FreightCar America's Long-Term Outlook

FreightCar America is poised for strong growth as the company operates as a key producer of railcars in the industry. The macrotrends favor ongoing growth for railcar production over multiple years. I expect FreightCar America to achieve steady long-term growth as its customers demand the innovative railcars that the company produces.

Analysts have a one-year price target of $11.83 for the stock, which is 32% above the current price. This target can be achieved from the expected gain in adjusted EBITDA growth plus from the expansion of the low the EV/EBITDA ratio. Continued growth in the backlog and margin increases should also help drive positive sentiment for the stock.

data.title: Upstart: An AI Fintech Gem
data.description:
Upstart Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:UPST) is at the intersection of two exciting fields. One is fintech and the other is AI. The combination of these two technologies promises strong growth and Upstart is positioning itself to be a significant player in this space. Investors have clearly been optimistic about the company's prospects as 52-week gains currently stand at around 220% despite giving back some gains since mid-February. In the below analysis, I determine there may be room to run. Performance was strong across their segments and the valuation is not overly expensive for a company that has such strong growth. Therefore, I initiate Upstart at a buy rating.

Financial Overview

Upstart reported its 2025 Q1 earnings back in early May. As you can see above, they generated total revenues of $213 million. This was down slightly from their Q4 but the YoY gains of 67% are promising in my view. Their Q1 represents the third straight quarter of revenue growth improvement, and so I believe it is clear that their business is overall on a great trajectory. This revenue figure also beat expectations as they surpassed estimates by $12.11 million. Therefore, they were able to outperform even when hopes were already high.

Upstart generated the amount of revenue shown above from originations (transaction volume) of $2.1 billion. This origination figure was up an impressive 89% YoY, also the third straight quarter of growth improvement. Business activity is therefore strengthening at an impressive pace in my view. If the Fed cuts rates a few times this year, this could further boost their business activity moving into year-end.

Upstart now has three straight quarters of being adjusted EBITDA positive as they reported $43 million in their Q1. This is a massive improvement from the year ago quarter's $20 million adjusted EBITDA loss. Lastly, adjusted EPS was reported to be $0.30, another major improvement from a $0.31 loss per share in the prior year period. As a whole, I believe this financial overview shows that Upstart is growing in a healthy manner. Next, let's take a look at how their segments performed.

Strength Across Segments

Personal loan originations hit $2.032 billion, up 83% YoY. This is the lowest growth rate among their segments. However, do note this is by far their largest segment and so the larger growth base is a valid explanation for this relatively lower growth rate. A growth rate is in the 80s percentage range and definitely cannot be complained about, and it is actually an acceleration from Q4's 65% growth rate. Note that # of loans increased around 100%, so clients seem to be taking out slightly smaller loans. Nonetheless, I believe these results show that their core business is performing in a strong manner.

Auto originations were highly impressive even though the growth base is a lot smaller than personal loans. They reported $61 million, up 42% QoQ and 369% YoY. Similar to personal loans, growth of originations accelerated from their Q4's 231% and so this business is becoming increasingly strong as well. Unlike personal loans, however, # of loans increased less than originations as they reported 356% YoY growth. This shows that the size of auto loans has increased. This is quite a surprise for me as I expected low consumer confidence to weigh on spending. In my view, these strong results amid headwinds clearly show demand for Upstart's services is rock-solid.

Their home segment saw the strongest growth figures but had the smallest growth base. They reported originations of $41 million, up 52% QoQ and 486% YoY. This is a slowdown from Q4's 800% growth, but I don't believe this comparison is too meaningful with 2023 Q4 originations being only $3 million. Their # of loans for this segment grew by 500% and so loan size was slightly smaller like in their personal loans segment. As a whole, I believe the strong results from all three segments show that Upstart is experiencing impressive widespread strength in their business especially with consumer confidence being low in the U.S.

Innovation Is Paying Off

Above is a diagram explaining how their innovation in automation is driving the company's success. As you can see above, automation is resulting in higher loan volumes for Upstart. The above metrics show that the company has good reasons for betting big on AI. 75% of their loans are approved automatically, and automated approvals are getting converted to loans at more than three times the rate of manually reviewed applications. This resulted in 92% of their 241K loans being completely automated. This suggests to me that Upstart is experiencing significant cost savings and improved business volume. I believe these figures show that their investments are paying off and that continued innovation will lead to further strength in their business down the road.

Outlook Is Bright

Upstart provided guidance for both the full year and Q2. Let's start with a discussion on select annual figures. They are expecting to see revenues of around $1.01 billion in 2025. This implies a 59% increase from 2024's $637 million figure. This is also a massive acceleration of growth from 2024's 24% growth rate and shows they are on the right path in annual terms. However, since Q1's growth rate was 67%, the next three quarters will be weaker than their first quarter on average. For adjusted EBITDA margin, they are expecting 19% for the full year. Note that this figure was only 2% for 2024 and so there has been a massive jump in profitability. Q1 results were in line with this full-year guidance as they reported a 20% margin. Overall, when zooming out and looking at the annual figures, I believe it is clear that the long-term growth direction is strong.

For their Q2 specifically, Upstart is projecting revenues of $225 million. This would represent a 76% growth rate from 2024 Q2's $128 million figure. This is an acceleration from Q1's already impressive 67% growth. As discussed above, note that full-year revenue growth is only expected to be 59% and so the second half of 2025 will therefore be weaker overall. For adjusted EBITDA, they are expecting to see $37 million, a major improvement from an adjusted EBITDA loss of $9.3 million in the prior year period. However, this improvement is less impressive than in Q1. Recall that for Q1, adjusted EBITDA improved from -$20 million to +$43 million YoY. Still, I would say Q2 guidance is highly respectable and shows that strength is expected to continue.

Room For Multiple Expansion

The P/S ratio looks like it has been on a roller coaster ride since late 2024. After hitting a low this year of slightly above 4.0, the multiple has rebounded rather swiftly to a reading of 9.424. While the valuation doesn't seem cheap, I believe there is room for further expansion. Early in this article, it was stated that revenue growth has accelerated for three straight quarters and is now at an impressive level. In the guidance section above, it is shown that there may be some modest cooling off in the second half of 2025 but not before another growth acceleration is expected in their Q2. In addition, full-year guidance shows that 2025 will be a major growth acceleration from 2024 and so their business is experiencing impressive momentum. While the P/S ratio is above its three-year average currently, I believe the solid Q1 results in all segments and the bright outlook conveyed by guidance should help to support a fresh multiyear high in the multiple. I'm well aware that this stock is not cheap. The Financials sector currently has a median P/S of only 3.15. However, I believe Upstart's strong innovation in Fintech AI justifies this valuation gap as their investments in technology are driving outstanding levels of growth. Again, I would not be surprised to see multiple expansion from current levels.

Bulls Dominate Near-Term

The 50-day SMA had a death cross with the 200-day SMA back in late April but long-term bearish momentum is now clearly receding with the deviation between the SMAs decreasing. Note that the stock broke back above the 200-day SMA in June as a major signal of strength. A golden cross seems possible in the not-so-distant future. In recent days, the stock has rallied above the Bollinger Bands upper band, so Upstart may be near-term overbought. Some cooling down should therefore not be ruled out in the near future. For the MACD, a bullish crossover occurred back at the start of June and while there have been periods of weakening momentum since then, the fact that the latest three histogram bars are green shows that the bulls are in control. The RSI echos that a current reading of 76.15 shows that the bulls are, in fact, crushing the bears currently. Lastly, note that the MACD and RSI have made fresh six-month highs recently and so the recent surge for the stock is confirmed by these indicators.

Concluding Remarks

I'm impressed with Upstart's position in the Fintech race. Their financial results show that the company is experiencing impressive growth and strength in all of their segments. Their entire business seems to be performing well. As discussed earlier, it is clear that their innovative AI solutions have driven growth and efficiency for their business. While the stock is definitely not cheap relative to the Financials sector, I believe their outstanding growth and technological advantages justify the elevated P/S ratio. In fact, there's likely room for expansion as the multiple sits below multiyear highs. Lastly, the above technical analysis shows that the bulls are in firm control. I believe these reasons are enough for me to conclude that Upstart is a buy at current levels.

data.title: Sezzle: Facing Deep BNPL Repricing
data.description:
Repricing Risk In BNPL

We initiate coverage on Sezzle Inc. (NASDAQ:SEZL) with a Strong Sell rating and $120 PT. Sezzle Inc. operates a digital BNPL platform that allows consumers in the US/CAD to split purchases into four interest-free installments online and offline. Its rapid growth, driven by a pivot to recurring revenue and an enterprise merchant segment, puts it at the center of this market’s recalibration. Despite consensus optimism (SEZL has been a perpetual upside surprise and our FY26E EPS of $4.60 is 8.3% ahead of the Street), we believe investors are overlooking a fundamental premise shift in the macro and regulatory backdrops for high-multiple fintech.

Valuations that once supported >53x forward P/E for names with rapid transaction volume growth and robust unit economics (70% margin; credit losses now controlled in the 2.5–3.0% range) made sense in the low-rate, free-capital regime that has passed. Today, persistent real rates and unprecedented state-level regulatory escalation signal an ongoing clampdown on multiple expansion, rising compliance costs and operational friction, especially as New York’s BNPL statute sets a precedent.

Peer median benchmarks for payments and BNPL signal a far lower sustainable valuation anchor, and our justified 26x FY26E P/E implies a 31% downside, even assuming high execution. Subscription monetization and buybacks offer some durability, but a macro or credit surprise to the upside that would drive a rapid snap-back is unlikely. We see R/R as heavily skewed negative as repricing gravity reasserts and the stock leaves little margin for error on macro.

Multiple Compression

We see acute multiple compression risk that the market continues to underestimate. Despite industry-leading margin performance and distinguished credit loss discipline, the market’s history of systematically de-rating high-multiple fintech as macro liquidity tightens and regulatory velocity accelerates is stark. SEZL traded more than 53 times forward P/E through much of the last cycle—a premium that industry-leading GMV expansion and contained loss profile could arguably justify in an abnormally supportive environment that today is obsolete. Forward sector medians have reset below 30 times for payments and digital lending, with high-growth platforms coalescing at or below that new ceiling as capital costs normalize. We benchmark SEZL’s implied valuation to AFRM and APT, each down more than 40 percent year over year from peak multiples over the past nine months as rate expectations and regulatory action converge.

Investor sentiment around SEZL’s recurring take-rate improvements and multi-year EPS outperformance fails to incorporate embedded cost of capital and statutory compliance risks as structural rather than transitory. We estimate a plus 50 basis points dispersion in discount rate assumptions alone—reasonable if US real yields remain sticky—implies an eighteen dollars per share decline in justified fair value assuming no operational misstep. As state-level buy now, pay later policies multiply and enforcement scope widens materially, we see the current multiple embedding an unsustainable scarcity premium and conclude that the path to re-rating lower is both plausible and urgent. The margin for error is vanishingly thin as the market’s tolerance for fintech growth stories reverts to a more skeptical baseline.

A Tightening Regulatory Clamp

We believe the regulatory and credit environment facing SEZL is set to materially worsen, with market impact underappreciated. The acceleration of state-led buy now, pay later (BNPL) legislation introduces hard compliance costs and operational friction, driving structural expenses higher while eroding runway for consumer underwriting innovation. New York’s statute requires full registration as a licensed lender and carries advance disclosures that extend well beyond Consumer Financial Protection Bureau guidance, shifting the landscape from light-touch oversight to a prescriptive, rules-based framework. As other high-population states consider analogous bills, enforcement risk grows since requirements vary by jurisdiction, amplifying resource burdens for compliance maintenance. Our channel checks suggest legal spend, audit fees, and systems retrofits could amount to as much as 90bps of annualized revenue in the next fiscal year, effectively absorbing 33% of SEZL’s recent operating margin gains.

Meanwhile, regulatory pressure directly erodes pricing leverage on late fees and non-standard merchant terms, which we estimate contributed 21% of adjusted EBITDA over the trailing 12 months. On the credit front, we see delinquency rates at the edge of the company’s stated tolerance band with net charge-offs rising quarter over quarter in the US even after tightening borrower screens. This twin squeeze of structural costs and deteriorating optics on credit risk challenges the sustainability of SEZL’s model. In summary, we see no realistic scenario in which compliance costs and credit risk move concertedly has been priced in. In light of these factors, we reiterate our Strong Sell rating.

Above-consensus EPS trajectory supported by sustainable drivers

We model Sezzle’s FY25E diluted EPS at 2.8% above Street consensus and estimate FY26E EPS at 8.3% above Street. This reflects a materially accelerated transition to recurring higher-margin revenues and outsized GMV growth. Unlike the Street, we incorporate a 62% GMV increase in FY25E and 40% in FY26E based on 77% underlying MODS growth and an enterprise ramp.

Source: FMP FY25 FY26
Estimate # Analysts Estimate # Analysts
Revenue $441.77M 2 $553.33M 3
EBITDA $-74.46M 2 $-93.27M 3
Net Income $111.07M 2 $143.44M 3
EPS $3.26 2 $4.25 3
SG&A Expenses $261.10M 2 $327.03M 3

Our confidence is bolstered by a stable 13% take rate and UEM at 70%, with PLR operating within management’s 2.5-3.0% range. Importantly, the WebBank program provides a full-year yield tailwind in FY25E vs. 2024. Our model includes subscription and ODD products reaching 35% of revenue in FY25E (42% in FY26E), driving operating leverage. Capital returns via share buybacks offset dilution, adding $0.06 to FY25E EPS vs. no buyback. Given Sezzle’s consistent track record of material beats to consensus (133% average beat over the last three quarters), we believe our above-consensus outlook is well-founded.

Valuation

Our $120 PT for SEZL is based on a 26x forward FY26E P/E multiple applied to our $4.60 EPS estimate. We view this as a balanced multiple that fairly assesses SEZL’s best-in-class 37.3% earnings growth profile and robust margins against a more conservative outlook on macro and regulatory headwinds in a higher-for-longer real rates environment and state-level scrutiny weighing on fintech multiples.

While fundamentals justify a premium versus SEZL’s historical average multiple of 19.8x and peer median of 14.6x, we believe significant contraction from the 53x level at the 100th percentile of its own and sector valuation bands is warranted, particularly as rising insider selling and a valuation regime that no longer rewards rapid top-line growth with frothy multiples prevail. Our target multiple reflects a premium for SEZL’s best-in-class recurring revenue mix and buyback-supported capital discipline, yet also incorporates repricing risk as investors recalibrate to a regime where capital is no longer costless. Put simply, the current multiple allows for minimal error, and our view that strong execution will not offset the downward pull on multiples leaves the R/R heavily in the bears’ favor.

Downside To Our Strong Sell Call

While we believe our Strong Sell thesis is largely predicated on unavoidable fintech repricing and regulatory risk, we acknowledge material upside scenarios. The most salient would be continued top- and bottom-line beat-and-raise execution driven by product innovation and recurring revenue scale (i.e., 123% y/y top-line growth, 77% y/y increase in transaction volume, and a net income margin above 34% in the first quarter of fiscal year 2025 results all suggest a company that could continue to inflect positively if momentum is sustained).

Ongoing credit outperformance (i.e., unit credit losses remaining below the low end of the management target band of 2.5–3.0%, despite sector stress) or a more benign macro environment than modeled could also provide multiple relief and a quicker rebound. We also see a scenario in which litigation tailwinds or regulatory outcomes are surprisingly favorable and accelerate merchant onboarding, and even more so one where Sezzle is able to capitalize on buy-now-pay-later shocks to the broader ecosystem (e.g., the Shopify antitrust case). In these circumstances, combined with further operating leverage from subscription or capital return programs, the stock could command a valuation premium for longer than our base case and potentially warrant reconsideration of our bearish view.

Conclusion

We see Sezzle’s current valuation as fundamentally mispricing the repricing risk facing high-growth fintechs in structurally higher rate, yet increasingly regulated, macro and regulatory regimes. Outstanding execution and recurring revenue momentum make SEZL an operational outlier, but we take these factors as already more than priced into a multiple that looks increasingly devoid of macro or regulatory disappointment margin. For investors, we see risk/reward as skewed to the downside, with the best path to value realization being persistent outperformance despite valuation compression, and recommend avoiding or actively reducing exposure as the valuation regime normalizes and repricing gravity resumes.

data.title: AGNC Investment Corp.: Mirror, Mirror On The Wall, Who's The Biggest Seller Of All?
data.description:
Sometimes investing thesis is fairly straightforward. All you need to do is look at the big picture facts, and you have your story. AGNC Investment Corp. (NASDAQ:AGNC) fits that well. The company is a mortgage REIT and one with a very simple story. We go over it in 3 parts and tell you where the real value lies.

1) Extremely Poor Returns, Except For A Brief Period

Over the last 10 years, the typical investor who buys mortgage REITs for income, has seen a rather painful time. AGNC has been no different. With a starting price of $18.82, your shares dropped almost exactly 50% since July 2015. Your total return if you did not reinvest the dividends was about 3.785% annually. If you reinvested your dividends (assuming you paid zero taxes), your total return was 5.455% annually.

Yes, we have all seen those extremely long-term charts of AGNC doing as well as the S&P. That entire return came during the 2008 and 2013 timeframe for those not reinvesting their dividends. If you put $1,000 into AGNC in April 2013 at $32.79 a share, today your total investment including dividends received (but not reinvested) would be $1,027.

So that is a rounding error of a return over 12 years. Mind you, if you reinvested, you still did horribly (2.7% compounded), but just a bit better. One other point here is that AGNC was doling out $5.00 of dividends in 2013 ($1.25 quarterly).

Today you are down to $1.44 (12 cents monthly). So your yield on cost has dropped under 3%.

One of the big investing lessons is that companies that provide extremely volatile returns, make poor investments. AGNC did do very well, once upon a time. But since then, they have provided a great number of trades, but very little for the buy-and-hold investor.

2) Stock Issuance Saves The Day

If you are a science fiction buff and know your multiverse, you would know that in an alternate Universe, AGNC returned 6,700%. But in this Universe, AGNC's share count is up by that much.

The share count has doubled in the last 3 odd years.

These are big numbers. AGNC has been able to sell so many shares at a premium to their tangible book over the last decade, and it has really helped boost their tangible equity per share. In other words, even their poor returns have been set up by the market constantly valuing AGNC at a premium. Imagine if the premium disappears one day. After all, why are you paying a premium for a stock that has done this poorly. Even within the sector, there are better choices.

3) With AGNC, You Are Not Buying Cheap Mortgage-Backed Securities

The line investors have been sold over the last few years is quite compelling.

Mortgage-backed securities are depressed. You want to buy here as the spreads to treasuries revert over time.

But you are not buying cheap mortgage-backed securities.

You are buying the mortgage-backed securities with 7.5X leverage.

That's the real "hook". If you want cheap mortgage backed securities, there are plenty of ways to play that. There are at least 10 ETFs that engage in this space, including Simplify MBS ETF (MTBA). Unfortunately, none of them have the allure of AGNC as none of them yield 15%. They don't yield 15% because none of them use 7.5X leverage on equity. AGNC has to hedge in multiple directions to try to make this work. This includes shorter-term rate movements, longer-term rate movements, and spreads between mortgage-backed securities and treasuries. Here is a sample of where they stand today.

If all of this sounds easy, then remember there is a clear reason that AGNC has not made money over the last 12 years. That reason is that this is very, very difficult to do. Sure, you could hedge everything at every single moment. But then you would make no money. The general idea is to leave some room to make money but if the market moves against you, 7.5X leverage adds up pretty fast.

Verdict

There are those waiting for the undervalued mortgage assets to create appreciation for them. As seen below, AGNC's share selling means that the assets per share have come down quite a lot. Whatever upside you think was, there is, it is constantly watered down.

Of course, AGNC and other mortgage REITs could make a lot of money in the future if the conditions are right. While we are one of the harshest critics of this asset class, even we have to admit that is possible. One outcome over the next few years is exactly that. Mortgage REITs could deliver spectacular returns. But the setup is not yet there, and we think it is a story that could start from far lower levels in AGNC. We remain on the lookout for that setup.

In the interim, investors can get the same yield, with better total returns via AGNC's preferred shares.

AGNC has five listed preferred shares.

  1. AGNC Investment Corp. 6.12 DP SH PFD F (NASDAQ:AGNCP).
  2. AGNC Investment Corp. 6.5% DP SH PFD E (NASDAQ:AGNCO).
  3. AGNC Investment Corp. 6.875 DEP REP D (NASDAQ:AGNCM).
  4. AGNC Investment Corp. CUM 1/1000 7% C (AGNCN).
  5. AGNC Investment Corp. 7.75% DP PFD G (NASDAQ:AGNCL).

Depending on which is the best value on the day (based on sound analysis and your outlook for rates), you can pick one with an identical "yield" to AGNC common shares by selling a small fraction of shares periodically. As we have previously demonstrated, this method produces far better total returns than buying and holding AGNC shares for yield. So same yield with better total returns. Who can say no to that? Oh yes, the guy in the picture above.

Please note that this is not financial advice. It may seem like it, sound like it, but surprisingly, it is not. Investors are expected to do their own due diligence and consult a professional who knows their objectives and constraints. Most of our articles are not written for "buy and hold" investors. Our articles are definitely not written for investors who ignore capital preservation while focusing only on yield.

data.title: e.l.f. Beauty: Rhode Acquisition Is A Huge Catalyst (Rating Upgrade)
data.description:
Introduction & Investment Thesis

When I last wrote about e.l.f. Beauty (ELF), I reiterated my “hold” rating, as I believed that the management had a lot of moving parts to navigate through in the short to midterm to build back investor confidence in the company.

I had said that while the company was fundamentally on the right track, as it had been gaining market share in Cosmetics and Skincare, while expanding internationally, it would require the following checklist to be met in order for me to turn bullish.

  1. Management would need to raise their top and bottom-line estimates for FY26 above what consensus estimates were pricing.

  2. Communicate the potential impact of the volatile tariff environment on their business.

  3. Lay forth a transparent action plan on how they plan to navigate the environment with levers such as price hikes, international expansion, and more.

When e.l.f. Beauty reported their Q4 FY25 earnings, the management did not provide forward FY26 guidance. However, they announced the acquisition of Rhode, a high-growth beauty brand founded by Hailey Rhode Bieber, which had clocked in total sales of $212M over the past twelve months, exclusively through DTC (direct-to-consumer) channels.

With the acquisition of Rhode, the management expects it to contribute to revenue from Q2 FY26 onwards, as they plan to broaden its product assortment in the skincare and hybrid makeup categories while simultaneously investing in marketing to expand Rhode’s brand awareness and retail distribution, thus leading to forward revenue estimates jumping higher.

At the same time, the management communicated that the estimated impact from tariffs would be $50M annualized cost to goods at current rates, given that 75% of their global production comes from China.

Finally, the management also laid forth their tariff mitigation strategy that would include 1) raising prices by $1 across their entire global product portfolio, starting August 1; 2) diversifying their supply chain; and 3) expanding internationally.

Although the impact of price increases on the consumer demand landscape is uncertain, along with the possibility that the $50M tariff headwind could be higher should tariff rates go up from current levels, I believe investor confidence has improved significantly as management met all three checklist requirements above.

As a result, I will upgrade my rating to a “buy” at current levels with a price target of $160 per share, representing an upside of 22% from its current levels.

Marking The 25th Consecutive Quarter Of Sales Growth With Improving Profitability

e.l.f. Beauty reported their Q4 FY25 earnings at the end of May, where revenue grew 4% YoY for the quarter to $332.6M, marking their 25th consecutive quarter of sales growth, while Adjusted EBITDA grew 99% YoY to $81.4M with a margin expansion of 1180 basis points to 24.5%.

During FY25, the company gained 190 basis points of market share in the US, 170 basis points of share in Canada, and 270 basis points of share in the UK as a result of their value proposition that is centered on driving quality product innovation at reasonable prices while leveraging their marketing engine to deepen customer loyalty.

When it comes to their Cosmetics segment, e.l.f. Beauty is the number one brand in the US by unit share and number two by dollar share, as they expand their retail presence in Target (TGT) and Walmart (WMT). Meanwhile, in Skincare, they expanded Naturium to Ulta Beauty (ULTA), which is already seeing strong results. Finally, it also continued to expand internationally, with revenues growing 19% YoY as the company gained market share in the UK and Canada, as well as in Germany, the Netherlands, Italy, and Mexico.

Mitigating Tariff Headwinds Through Pricing, Supply Chain Optimization, And International Expansion

For FY26, the management hasn’t provided forward guidance; however, during the earnings call, Tarang Amin, CEO of e.l.f. Beauty, discussed at length the impact of tariffs and their mitigation efforts in order to continue to deliver superior growth and market share gains compared to their peers.

First, about 75% of their global production comes from China. Per management commentary, product imports of the US were subject to tariffs at the 55% level (25% that was put in place in 2019, plus an incremental 30% that is now in place through mid-August), which would translate to a gross impact of $50M to their cost of goods sold on an annualized basis.

Second, in order to mitigate the impact of the tariffs, the management is taking action through three vectors, which include pricing, supply chain optimization, and business diversification.

When it comes to pricing, there will be a $1 increase on their entire product assortment globally, starting August 1. Like I said in my previous post, despite raising prices by $1, e.l.f. Beauty’s products will continue to be cheaper than its competitors, which could ultimately help the company gain greater market share, as consumers opt for quality at a reasonable price amid tightening budgets.

Meanwhile, on supply chain optimization, e.l.f. Beauty will work on reducing their dependence on China throughout FY26 as they work with key suppliers to set up additional operations while simultaneously pursuing cost savings and supplier concessions.

Finally, the third way in which e.l.f. Beauty plans on mitigating the impact of tariffs on its top and bottom lines is through international expansion, which is the fastest-growing part of their business. Over the last six years, international sales have grown from 10% to 20% of total revenue, as they gain market share in existing markets as well as expand into new markets.

Rhode Acquisition Could Be A Game Changer

Recently, e.l.f. Beauty signed an agreement to acquire Rhode, a fast-growing, multicategory lifestyle beauty brand founded by Hailey Bieber.

Similar to how the acquisition of Naturium in 2023 helped e.l.f. Beauty to double down on the white space in Skincare, the management believes that the Rhode acquisition will enable them to further transform the beauty industry by delivering high-quality innovation to engaged communities.

Over the last 12 months, Rhode has achieved $212M in net sales, which is DTC only with just 10 products.

With the acquisition, e.l.f. Beauty will further tighten its relationship with Gen Z customers, as they plan to broaden its product assortment in the skincare and hybrid makeup categories while simultaneously investing in marketing to expand Rhode’s brand awareness and retail distribution.

The management expects Rhode to contribute to the company’s results from Q2 FY26 onwards. This has already led to analysts revising their forward revenue estimates higher, as can be seen below.

Meanwhile, Mandy Fields, CFO at e.l.f. Beauty, discussed the Rhode acquisition structure, where the $1B headline price breaks out into two components. First, the upfront purchase price of $800M represents a premium of 3.8 times Rhode’s last 12 months of sales, which will be financed using $600M of debt financing and $200M of equity, that is, roughly 2.6M newly issued shares of e.l.f. Beauty common stock. Second, the transaction also includes additional potential earnout consideration of $200M based on the future growth of the brand over a three-year timeframe.

The way I see it, amid tariff uncertainty, the acquisition of Rhode will add a long-term growth lever to e.l.f. Beauty as the management mitigates short-term risk through pricing, supply chain optimization, and international expansion.

Revisiting My Valuation

Looking forward, I will take the latest consensus estimates into account, where revenue is expected to grow in the higher twenties range in FY26 to $1.66B, followed by growth in the high teens in FY27 and low to mid-teens in FY28 to a total of $2.19B. I believe this can be achieved as management successfully navigates beyond the short-term tariff uncertainty to gain market share in its Cosmetics, Skincare segment through a combination of robust product innovation and international market expansion. Plus, the integration of Rhode will add another growth lever as it tightens its relationship with the Gen Z customer cohort while expanding its product assortment and retail distribution.

From a profitability standpoint, e.l.f. Beauty is expected to see its earnings per share grow faster than revenue growth in the coming three years as a result of improving unit economics and targeted marketing investments to deepen loyalty among its existing and new customer base. As per consensus estimates, e.l.f. Beauty should generate $5.15 of earnings per share by FY28, which will be equivalent to a present value of $4.42 per share when discounted at 8%.

While the stock has gained back over 160% after hitting its trough in April, I believe the bottom is likely in. Although the management did not provide full-year guidance, I believe investors are looking beyond short-term noise to the company’s long-term growth picture, where it continues to demonstrate progress through product innovation, international expansion, and targeted acquisitions such as the one with Rhode.

Plus, as the management mitigates short-term tariff pressures through price increases, supply chain optimization, and business diversification, I believe it is well positioned to deliver positive surprises in its coming earnings calls, which could rerate its forward price-to-earnings multiple higher by at least 20% to a price target of $160 per share when benchmarked against the average S&P 500 multiple.

Risks And Conclusions

So far, management has been able to reinstill investor confidence with the Rhode acquisition, which should be a long-term growth lever. Plus, they have also clearly communicated that they estimate a $50M headwind to the cost of goods sold under current tariff rates while simultaneously laying out their tariff mitigation strategies.

While I believe that the bottom is likely in, the consumer demand landscape continues to be fragile. As a result, we could see units sold go down when price increases go into effect on August 1. The question is whether the price hikes will sufficiently offset the decline in unit volumes.

At the same time, the estimate of a $50M headwind to the cost of goods sold is under the current tariff rate. Should rates go higher or the mitigation strategies take longer to play out, particularly with supply chain optimization and international expansion, we could see renewed volatility in the stock price.

However, in all of this, what is optimistic, in my opinion, is that the management is taking a balanced approach towards ensuring long-term growth with the Rhode acquisition, while simultaneously mitigating short-term tariff headwinds to protect their top and bottom lines.

After assessing both the “good” and the “bad,” I will upgrade my rating to a “buy” with a price target of $160 per share, as I believe that the worst is likely behind us, as the management is successfully navigating short-term headwinds while positioning the company for long-term success.

data.title: EV Company News For The Month Of June 2025
data.description:
Welcome to the June 2025 edition of Electric Vehicle (EV) company news.

The past month saw a strong month of EV sales reported for May.

Plugin electric car sales as of end of May 2025

Global plugin electric car sales were over 1.6 million in May 2025, up 22% on May 2024 sales. Global plugin electric car market share in May was 25% and 22% YTD (for 2025).

China's plugin electric car sales were over 1 million in May 2025, up ~23% on May 2024 sales. The plugin electric car market share in China for May was 53%, and 49% YTD (for 2025).

Europe's plugin electric car sales were 305,000 in May 2025, up 34% YoY, reaching 28% market share and 26% YTD (for 2025). Norway reached 94.9% share, Sweden 63.2%, Germany 28.5%, France 22.3%, and the UK 33.7% in May 2025.

USA's plugin electric car sales were 131,062 (as reported by Argonne National Laboratory) in May 2025, down 2.3% on May 2024 sales. The plugin electric car market share in the USA for May was 8.94%.

Australia's plugin electric car sales market share was 12% for May 2025.

Note: Globally, 100% battery electric cars represented 66% market share YTD (as of end May 2025) of all plugin electric cars sold. The above sales include light commercial vehicles.

Note: An acknowledgement to CleanTechnica for their work compiling most of the electric car sales data quoted above and in the article below.

Top-selling global plugin electric car groups YTD (as of end May 2025) (source)

Global EV sales forecasts in the years ahead

BloombergNEF is bullish on 2025 global plugin EV sales, forecasting ~22 million sales and 30% YoY growth (source)

EV Volumes forecasts global plugin electric car sales to reach 45% share & 46m sales in 2030 (source)(forecast as of early 2024)

The IEA states: "Global electric light-duty vehicle sales are set to reach 40% in 2030 and almost 55% in 2035 based on current policy settings."

On January 28, 2025, Reuters reported:

EV car sales to top 20 million in 2025, a research firm says. Chinese brands are set to account for nearly two-thirds of all EV deliveries worldwide... research firm Rho Motion forecast on Tuesday.

Seeking Alpha's Trend Investing global electric plugin car sales forecast

Sales (million) Market share
2025 21.3 24%
2030 46 51%
2035 73 81%

Trend Investing comment

We are still only at the extremely early stage of the EV boom — We have still only electrified < 10% of the global fleet of 1.475 billion, with a staggering ~90%+ still to go.

Trend Investing's global plugin electric car sales forecast to 2040 (green bars)

EV market news

On June 6, Seeking Alpha reported:

Chinese authorities caution BYD, rivals to self-regulate as price war intensifies - report... The authorities urged automakers to self-regulate and avoid selling vehicles below cost or offering excessive discounts. They warned that such practices could lead to unsustainable competition, financial strain across the supply chain, and potential bankruptcies of weaker firms. The government also highlighted issues such as “zero-mileage” cars and mounting debts that are straining cash flows.

On June 11, CNEVPOST reported:

Chinese automakers, including BYD, and Geely, pledge to shorten payment cycles to suppliers... A total of 17 automakers, including BYD, Geely Auto, Chery, and Xpeng, have announced this commitment... they will commit to shortening supplier payment terms to within 60 days.

On June 14, Bloomberg reported:

US to ease rules hindering Tesla’s self-driving Cybercab. The Trump administration is taking steps that would make it easier for automakers to deploy self-driving cars without driver controls, a potential boon to the ambitions of Tesla Inc. and rivals looking to put robotaxis on US roads in the near future. Current rules require automakers that want to deploy self-driving cars designed without a steering wheel or brake pedals to seek an exemption from federal safety standards that effectively require that new cars have human driving controls. The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration will “streamline” that exemption process...

In mid-June, CleanTechnica reported:

Brazil EV sales report: Local EV production imminent as sales reach a new record in May. With over 14,000 EVs sold in May (an all-time high) and year-on-year growth of 63%, Brazil’s EV market seems to be gearing up for a record 2025... This is happening while the country gets ready for local EV production: BYD and GWM will start churning out the Dolphin Mini and the Haval H6 in July, in their Camaçarí and Iracemápolis factories, respectively.

On June 20, WhichCar reported:

Geely boss' stark warning about global EV overcapacity amid China’s fierce price war... Li announced that Geely would refrain from building new manufacturing plants or expanding capacity at existing facilities, in a bid to avoid adding to the growing glut of vehicle production.

On June 21, Seeking Alpha reported: "Supreme Court ruling allows fuel producers to challenge California emissions standards."

On June 21, Reuters reported: "Chinese EV maker Hozon enters bankruptcy proceedings, state media reports."

On June 22, The Driven reported: "World’s biggest hydrogen taxi operator goes all electric, blames high costs."

On June 25, Car News China reported:

4 Tesla Model Y competitors to launch in the next 4 weeks in China... Four Chinese SUVs will be launched in the next four weeks to compete with the U.S. automaker’s SUVs. Starting this week, on June 26, the most serious Model Y challenger will hit the Chinese market—the Xiaomi YU7.

On June 25, Seeking Alpha reported: "Federal judge blocks Trump admin from withholding EV charging funds."

EV company news

BYD Co. [SHE: 002594][HK:1211](OTCPK:BYDDY), Yangwang, Denza (OTCPK:OTCPK:BYDDF)

BYD is currently ranked the number 1 globally with 24.3% market share YTD (as of end May 2025). BYD is ranked number 1 in China with 28.9% market share YTD (as of end May 2025).

On June 6, CNEVPOST reported:

BYD to nearly triple its dealer network in South Africa by 2026. BYD plans to expand its dealer network in South Africa to 20 stores by the end of this year and increase it to 30 to 35 stores by 2026.

On June 7, CNEVPOST reported:

BYD launches Seal 06 EV sedan, starting at $15,270. The Seal 06 EV is BYD's latest affordable electric sedan, with a range of up to 545 km... and is equipped with BYD's God's Eye C smart driving system.

On June 12, Seeking Alpha reported:

BYD brings affordable compact EV to the UK, intensifying competition. BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) launched its most affordable and compact electric vehicle in the UK—Dolphin Surf—intensifying competition in the small electric hatchback segment. The pricing is expected to start at £18,650 in the UK and is the British equivalent of BYD’s popular Seagull hatchback.

On June 18, CNEVPOST reported:

BYD partners with China's top 2 EV charger operators to build 1-MW ultra-fast chargers. BYD has teamed up with Teld and Star Charge to build 1-MW ultra-fast chargers. At the end of last month, BYD partnered with two more charger operators to jointly build 15,000 1-MW chargers.

On June 20, CNEVPOST reported: "BYD to launch Sealion 06 SUV in late July, offering hybrid and pure electric variants, according to a local media report."

On June 24, CNEVPOST reported: "BYD Xi'an to carry 7,000 cars to Europe on maiden voyage."

On June 26, CNEVPOST reported:

Exclusive: BYD's production capacity remains stable, says source close to NEV maker... BYD dealers' inventory levels are also within reasonable ranges, the source added.

On June 26, Electrek reported:

BYD denies rumors of cutting EV production, says sales are still growing steadily. With nearly 382,476 new energy vehicles (NEVs) sold globally in May, BYD is coming off its best sales month of 2025... Not including its commercial vehicles, BYD’s passenger vehicle sales are up 37% through May, with over 1.73 million units sold... BYD still expects to sell around 5.5 million vehicles this year, a nearly 30% increase from 2024.

Geely Automobile Holdings Ltd. (OTCPK:GELYY, HK:0175), Volvo Cars, Polestar (PSNY), Kandi Technologies Group (NASDAQ:KNDI), Proton, Lotus, Zeekr (ZK) (also owns Lynk), and Farizon. (Note: Volvo Group is a separate company that makes e-trucks & e-buses)

Geely-Volvo is currently ranked number 2 in the global electric car manufacturers' sales ranking with 11.3% global market share YTD (as of end May 2025). Geely-Volvo is the number 2 ranked in China with 13.1% market share YTD (as of end May 2025). Geely-Volvo is 5th ranked in Europe with 7.7% market share YTD (as of end May 2025).

On June 1, Geely announced:

Geely Automobile Holdings Limited: TOTAL SALES VOLUME FOR MAY 2025 WAS 235,208 UNITS, TOTAL SALES VOLUME UP 46% YOY.

Note: Geely Group BEV sales for May were 95,040, up 178% YoY; PHEV sales for May were 42,981, up 76% YoY.

On June 3, CNEVPOST reported: "Zeekr to launch Zeekr 7X SUV in Hong Kong later this month."

On June 20, Car News China reported:

Geely’s troubled brand Livan prepared an answer to SGMW’s Wuling Mini EV... the Livan Smurf two-door hatchback based on the previously launched Geely Panda Mini EV. Named after a well-known Belgian comic franchise, this EV has a body length of 3,1 meters and a peak power of 40 hp... This brand focuses on manufacturing cars with swappable batteries for taxi services in China. However, its sales volume isn’t high, with 13,224 units sold from January to May 2025.

On June 26, Car News China reported: "Geely EX5 electric SUV officially enters the Greek market."

Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA)

Tesla is currently ranked the number 3 globally with 7.4% global market YTD (as of the end of May 2025). Tesla is number 5 in China with 4.6% market share YTD (as of the end of May 2025). Tesla is no longer ranked in the top 5 in Europe (as of the end of May 2025). Tesla is the number 1 electric car seller in the U.S. with 43% of sales in Q1, 2025.

On June 13, Seeking Alpha reported: "Tesla unveils upgraded Model S and X in U.S. with $5,000 price hike... in the U.S."

On June 20, Reuters reported:

Tesla, Shanghai sign $557 million energy storage station deal, Yicai reports. Tesla's first China grid-scale battery storage station using its megapack batteries will be located in Shanghai, local media Yicai reported on Friday. The deal involving an investment of 4 billion yuan ($556.8 million) was signed between the U.S. automaker, China Kangfu International Leasing Co., and the Shanghai local government on Friday, according to the report.

On June 22, Seeking Alpha reported:

Tesla launches a limited robotaxi service in Austin on a landmark day for autonomous ambitions... to mark a significant milestone in the company’s goal to become the autonomous driving leader... the initial rollout is highly controlled and invite-only, with access granted primarily to select Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) enthusiasts, investors, and social media influencers. The robotaxi service will use Tesla (TSLA) Model Y vehicles equipped with an advanced version of the company’s Full Self-Driving software.

On June 23, Seeking Alpha reported: "Tesla jumps 10% after the limited robotaxi launch generates positive reviews."

On June 23, Bloomberg reported: "Tesla Robotaxi incidents draw scrutiny from US safety agency."

On June 25, Reuters reported:

Tesla's European sales slump for fifth month... Tesla's new car sales in Europe fell 27.9% in May from a year earlier, even as fully-electric vehicle sales in the region jumped 27.2%, with the U.S. EV maker's revised Model Y yet to show signs of reviving the brand's fortunes.

Volkswagen Group [Xetra:VOW](OTCPK:VWAGY) (OTCPK:VLKAF)/ Audi / Lamborghini/ Porsche (OTCPK:POAHF)/ Skoda/ Bentley

'Volkswagen Group' is currently ranked the number 4 top-selling global electric car manufacturer with 7.3% market share YTD (as of the end of May 2025), and 1st in Europe with 28.1% market share YTD (as of the end of May 2025).

On May 22, Autovista24 reported:

VW tops European EV market as overall demand rises... A total of 847,591 EVs took to European roads in the first quarter of 2025, an increase of 20% year on year.

On June 9, ZeCar reported: "Volkswagen expands EV lineup in Australia with new ID.4 GTX and ID.5 Pro."

SAIC Motor Corporation Limited [SAIC] [CH:600104] (SAIC includes Roewe, MG, Baojun, Maxus), Wuling Automobile JV (SAIC 51%, GM 44%, Guangxi 5.9%)

SGMW (SAIC-GM-Wuling Automobile) is number 5 globally with 5.5% market share YTD (as of the end of May 2025). SAIC/GM/Wulin JV [SGMW] plus SAIC is 4th in China with 6.3% share YTD (as of the end of May 2025).

On June 26, CNEVPOST reported:

SAIC's new car carrier joins fleet, bringing total to 37. Anji Commonality becomes SAIC's 37th car carrier and will head to Europe on its maiden voyage.

Chongqing Changan Automobile [SHE:000625] ("Changan") + Avatr Technology (backed by Changan, CATL, Huawei) + Deepal. Note: Changan is having advanced-stage merger talks with Dongfeng Motor Group Company Limited [HKSE:0489] (OTCPK:DNFGF)

On June 1, CleanTechnica reported:

Changan solid-state battery will unlock up to 1500 kilometers of range... Even CATL and BYD say they have solid-state batteries in the pipeline that they might begin producing in small batches by 2028. But Changan says its batteries will be in production by the end of this year, with verification and calibration procedures taking place next year. By 2027, it expects production cars using its solid-state batteries to be on the road in China.

BMW (OTCPK:BMWYY), Mini, Rolls-Royce

BMW Group is currently ranked the number 7 global electric car manufacturer with 3.6% global market share YTD (as of the end of May 2025). BMW Group is ranked 2nd in Europe with 10.6% market share YTD (as of the end of May 2025).

On June 10, BMWBlog reported:

Beyond the Vision 100: BMW’s road to the Neue Klasse era... In 2016, BMW built the ‘Vision 100’ concept, which offered us a glimpse of things to come... BMW emphasized the principles of Electrification, Digitalization, and Circularity with the ‘Vision 100’... BMW will also use an 800-volt electrical architecture for the powertrain... BMW’s latest generation of electrification – Gen6 – will use electrically excited synchronous motors (EESM) for RWD and on the rear axle on AWD vehicles. For potential FWD and AWD, the cars will use asynchronous motors (ASM) at the front. Energy losses are reduced by 40%, weight by 10%, and production costs are lowered by 20% compared to Gen5 tech... BMW will also use cylindrical battery cells in Gen6 electrification... The new batteries have a greater energy density, over 20%, than the current generation of batteries... The batteries use recycled lithium, cobalt, and nickel. The cost to manufacture the new batteries is subsequently up to 50% less than the old batteries... From the ‘Vision 100’ concept to the ‘Neue Klasse’, BMW has shown what it believes the future of ‘Joy of Driving’ will be.

Xiaomi YU7 hits the market as a competitor to the Tesla Model Y (source)

Nio signs new distributor deal to enter 3 more European markets.

Conclusion

May 2025 global plugin electric car sales were over 1.6 million, up 22% YoY, and reached 25% global market share; 53% share in China, 28% in Europe, and 8.94% in the USA.

Highlights for the month were:

  • Chinese authorities caution BYD, rivals to self-regulate as price war intensifies - report.
  • The Trump administration is taking steps to make it easier for automakers to deploy self-driving cars without driver controls.
  • Brazil: Local EV production imminent as sales reach a new record in May.
  • Geely boss' stark warning about global EV overcapacity amid China’s fierce price war.
  • Supreme Court ruling allows fuel producers to challenge California emissions standards.
  • Chinese EV maker Hozon enters bankruptcy proceedings - reports.
  • World’s biggest hydrogen taxi operator goes all electric, blames high costs.
  • 4 Tesla Model Y competitors to launch in the next 4 weeks in China.
  • BYD denies rumors of cutting EV production, says sales are still growing steadily.
  • Geely Group BEV sales for May were 95,040, up 178% YoY; PHEV sales for May were 42,981, up 76% YoY.
  • Tesla, Shanghai sign $557 million energy storage megapacks deal. Tesla launches a limited robotaxi service in Austin, Texas — receives strong reviews; however, draws scrutiny from the US safety agency.
  • VW tops the European EV market as overall demand rises. European Q1 EV sales up 20% YoY.
  • BMW discusses its latest generation of electrification – Gen6.
  • Kia releases details on the Kia EV4 — the brand’s first electric compact sedan.
  • Li Auto to launch Li i8, its first electric SUV, in late July.
  • Mercedes-AMG concept GT XX previews new electric car, with fake V8 sounds and 1000kW.
  • Xpeng launches X9 MPV in Indonesia, local assembly to begin in July. New versions of the XPENG G6 and G9 are bound for Europe.
  • Xiaomi's EV business is expected to turn profitable in H2 2025, the founder says. Xiaomi invested 87.5 million USD in Beijing for a new EV factory. SU7 hits the market, sells over 200,000 units in 3 minutes in China.
  • Nio signs new distributor deal to enter 3 more European markets, following last week's news to enter seven new European markets. Nio reports Q1 Net loss of RMB6,750.0M (US$930.2M), an increase of 30.2% from Q1 2024.
  • GAC Motor Co. has started assembling electric vehicles in Indonesia, accelerating an expansion in Southeast Asia.
  • Great Wall Motor sold 32,638 NEVs in May, up 32.41% YoY.
  • Ford's May Total electrified vehicle sales were 29,442, up 10.7% YoY.
  • General Motors plans to invest $4B in its US plants over the next 2 years.
  • Rivian eliminates 140 manufacturing jobs to reduce 'process inefficiencies'.
  • Huawei takes on Germany's big three automobile in China's luxury EV showdown; new Maextro S800 deliveries began on June 26.
